prose:
Fukui reactivity indices were computed for benzaldehyde (14 atoms) using DFT at the B3LYP/def2-TZVP level (tier 'standard') in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry was taken from the user-supplied .xyz file (not re-optimized at this level). Partial charges were computed via Mulliken (PySCF) for the neutral, cation (N−1, charge +1, mult 2), and anion (N+1, charge −1, mult 2) species; Fukui indices were obtained by finite difference.

Method provenance: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP, PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1.

Key results:
- Most electrophilic site (largest f+): Atom 3 (C), f+ = 0.39938963
- Most nucleophilic site (largest f−): Atom 1 (O), f− = 0.23839906

Full per-atom Fukui indices (f+, f−, f0, dual descriptor):

Integrity checks: sum(f+) = 1.0000 (pass), sum(f−) = 1.0000 (pass), schema header present (pass). Overall: status = ok, trustworthy = true. No warnings reported by the engine.
